title: lilium bulbiferum var. croceum
intro: Lilium bulbiferum var. croceum, also known as the Golden-Rayed Lily, is a variety of the Lilium bulbiferum species, characterized by its bright yellow flowers with golden anthers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins. The medicinal properties of Golden-Rayed Lily have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant is typically prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy.
